snippet: Disciplinary action consistent with the relevant industrial instrument and legislation, including dismissal, may be taken where school staff fail to adhere to this policy. This includes where school staff fail to avoid wherever possible, or identify, declare and manage a conflict of interest related to gifts, benefits and hospitality in accordance with the Conflict of Interest policy . Actions inconsistent with this policy may constitute misconduct under the Public Administration Act 2004, which includes: , such as sections of the codes covering conflict of interest, public trust and gifts and benefits school staff making improper use of their position. In the event of identified non-compliance, principals and managers may proceed with actions in accordance with the Managing Conduct and Unsatisfactory Performance in the Teaching Service policy . Procurement Division will report instances of potential non-compliance to authorised delegates, to allow them to take actions under the Managing Conduct and Unsatisfactory Performance in the Teaching Service policy. Instances of non-compliance will also be reported to the department’s Audit and Risk Committee.
